diagnosis: Atrial flutter with 2:1 block. Note that there are 2 atrial waves for each QRS in lead V1. The atrial rate is about 240; therefore, this could easily be misread as sinus tachycardia or paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia (PSVT) variant--not by you of course. ECG also shows left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH) and possible prior antero-septal myocardial infarction (MI), along with non-specific ST-T changes.
